questions.stem: Which of the following best describes material culture?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The physical objects and artifacts that members of a society create and use.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The process by which societies move toward industrial, urban, and rationalized forms.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The mechanisms a society uses to encourage or compel conformity to its norms.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The view that deviance arises when people cannot reach valued goals through legitimate means.

questions.explanation: Material culture is best described as the physical objects and artifacts that members of a society create and use. The other options describe different concepts.

hints.prompt: Sociologists divide culture into material culture and nonmaterial culture. This division is based on which distinction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Whether a cultural element is a tangible object or an intangible idea

choices.label: B

choices.label: C
choices.text: Whether a custom is old or newly invented

choices.label: D

hints.rationale: The material/nonmaterial split separates a society's physical creations from its intangible beliefs and ideas.

hints.prompt: A society's language, values, and religious beliefs belong to nonmaterial culture. Which of the following would instead count as material culture?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A shared moral rule against stealing

choices.label: B
choices.text: A traditional tool, coin, or building the society produces

choices.label: C
choices.text: A widely held political belief

choices.label: D
choices.text: A spoken dialect

hints.rationale: Material culture is the physical, tangible side of culture, unlike intangible beliefs, values, or language.

choices.label: B

choices.label: D
choices.text: Conclude that material culture cannot be described at all.
